meta.setting: High pine ridges, smoke-watch rocks, relay trails, and roads below French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with distance, warning, fire, weather, riders, and hard choices

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek land knowledge and fire stewardship context, handled respectfully.

research_grounding.title: Karuk Tribe
research_grounding.use: Cultural burning, environmental stewardship, and northern California land context.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Black Bear
research_grounding.use: Wildlife movement and forest habitat context for high-country observation.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Ridge Lookouts cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented smoke note, dust warning, or high-watch saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Smoke and Signal Watchers
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing high-country watchers who read smoke, dust, riders, weather, animal movement, and approaching trouble
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four terse signs or habits
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate ridge lookouts as practical observers: fire, riders, animal movement, weather shift, rescue warning, and public danger.
Tie future moves to signal timing, mistaken smoke, hidden approach, witness from afar, mirror flash, gunshot relay, and choosing who gets warned first.
Keep signals grounded: smoke columns, mirror flash, cloth, gunshot, shouted relay, and a rider sent downhill.
Acknowledge Native fire and land knowledge respectfully without inventing ceremony or secret code.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: They should feel like people paid in cold fingers and the power to be first with bad news. From a ridge, mercy and betrayal can look like the same dust plume.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A lookout scene should ask what is really moving below, who deserves warning, and who profits from a late signal.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western signal, ridge, fire, and road language only.
Keep lookout work practical, fallible, and socially consequential.
The generated page must include image path images/ridge-lookouts.jpg.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred-invented, or fake-tribal framing
do not make signals magical, perfectly reliable, or secret-sacred
do not use modern radios, binoculars if too modern for context, vehicles, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about smoke, dust, signals, distance, warning, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
